trend_name: May Your Bubble Ponytails Always Be as Chic as Oprah Winfrey's
description:
Why is she so iconic?

Oprah Winfreyis proving that she can be a front row chic, too. Along with nearly everyone else in Hollywood, Winfrey is currently atParis Fashion Week. On March 5, she was photographed at theChloé F/W '26runway show, where she wore wide leg blue jeans, a ruffled cream blouse, and acropped, tan jacket. As for her glam, she kept things casual with light makeup that included a filled-in brow and a glossy lip along with an updo, specifically a sleek, highbubble ponytailthat was equal parts elegant and relaxed.
Oprah Winfrey wears a bubble ponytail during Paris Fashion Week.
No matter where you're going or what mood you're in, you truly can't go wrong by pulling your hair up into aponytail, and throwing in a few extra elastics to give it the bubble look is an easy way to liven it up. Obviously, the ponytail has been a go-to hairstyle among women for ages, but last year, high-drama ponytails—from theBarbie ponytailto themessy ponytailto thebraided ponytail—reigned supreme.
"The power of the ponytail is that it can give structural angles as well as a snatch," celebrity hairstylistAdir Abergelpreviously toldMC. "The positioning, whether high, low, or mid, can have a transformative effect on the silhouette of not just the face but the entire body and the fashion being worn."
Celebrity hairstylistKellon Deryckhas similar thoughts. “It works across textures and occasions, which is why it’s become iconic,” he says. “It’s universal. But when styled with intention, it feels like high art.”
Ready to get the look? Read ahead for tips on how to achieve the ponytail of your dreams.
The best way to lay down flyaways and give yourself the sleekest finish possible is to brush your hair into place with a smoothing brush.
This is a light gel that can be used onfine hair typesto hold your ponytail in place.
This thick gel can hold thickertextured hairin place without flaking or being too heavy.
Don't forget the most important accessory of them all: an elastic that won't snag or pull on your hair.
Use a freeze spray to keep stray hairs down for good. If you're wearing a wraparound ponytail, you can also spray this at the base to keep the hair in place.
Get exclusive access to fashion and beauty trends, hot-off-the-press celebrity news, and more.
Danielle Jacksonis the senior beauty writer at Marie Claire. She has nearly a decade of experience covering beauty, lifestyle, and entertainment and was previously the senior beauty editor atWomen's Health, where she thoughtfully covered topics related to skincare, haircare, aesthetics, and wellness. Before that, she spent three years as an assistant beauty editor atPS,and in the years since,her work has appeared in titles likeVogue,InStyle,Glamour, and more. Danielle graduated from the University of Georgia with a BA in English, and has lived in Brooklyn for almost ten years. When she's not writing, you can find her reading romance novels and talking about sunscreen. You can find her on Instagram @danielleknecole.

trend_name: The Best Beauty Sales of the Week Include My Favorite Acne-Fighting Serum and Microcurrent Device
description:
Clearer, firmer skin is closer than you think with these Ulta and Dermstore finds.

This week, thebest beauty salesare about investments. With beauty brands constantly releasing new products, it can be hard to decide what high end items are actually worth investing in. Is itluxury beauty products? Or maybe a new high-endperfume? Perhaps you prefer to save withdrugstore skincareso you can splurge on aDyson hairdryer? However you manage your beauty budget, the one thing we can all agree on is that it's better to splurge when it's on sale. And this week's best beauty sales are what splurge dreams are made of.
BothUltaand Dermstore have major discounts this week, offering plenty of skincare products and high-endtoolson sale, including $130 off a Dyson Airwrap and $79 off aNuFace microcurrent device. Of course, if you're not looking to spend money on a beauty tool this week, there are also plenty of high-quality skincare and makeup products on sale, too. Personally, I'll be adding my absolute favorite azelaic acid serum and the bestnail strengtheneron the market (in my humble opinion) to my cart this weekend—just to name a few. Here are a few other products I've got my eye on that are worth the splurge.
Dermstore is currently having itsBeauty Refresh Sale, with up to 20 percent off select products using the code REFRESH.
This serum from famed plastic surgeon Dr. Diamond was namedMarie Claire's Best Serum Overallof 2025, and it's not hard to understand why. The dermatologist-beloved serum uses bioengineered human identical growth factors to stimulate your skin's ability to repair itself and promote firmer, more lifted looking skin.
The first time I tried this microcurrent facial toning device, I looked in the mirror and unironically said, “Oh wow, it's like I have a new face.” Needless to say, this little tool lives up to its name. Use it properly, and it'll make your entire face look lifted and, dare I say, brand new, in minutes.
Sunscreenis one of the only beauty products I wear every single day (other than my usual skincare routine), and this one from Supergoop! is what I go to when I don't want to worry about potential white cast or pilling. And thanks to the Dermstore sale, this jumbo-sized bottle gets you the most bang for your buck.
I haveacne-prone skinwith never-ending redness and uneven tone, so I'm always on the hunt for a good product withazelaic acid, which can help calm redness and fight discoloration without irritating the skin. This formula from Allies of Skin is my absolute favorite.
As I get older, I've noticed that the trick to a good makeup look is a good, hydratedunder-eye. This RMS Beauty concealer gives coverage without drying out my skin, leaving my under-eyes smooth instead of cakey. I also love that it can be sheered out or layered for added coverage.
If you're a regular at your local nail salon, odds are yourcuticlesand nails could use some TLC. Enter: ISDIN's Si-Nails Strengthening Nail Serum. The formula helps boost keratin production, which in turn leads to stronger, healthier looking nails. Plus the pen packaging makes it easy to use on the go.
If you're an Ulta member, you can get 20 percent off select hair tools by simply entering thecode HAIR20. The code will be valid through 3/7/26.
The Revlon Hot Air Brush is a classic for a reason. This updated version is just as good as the viral original if you're looking for a 2-in-1 tool that'll dry your hair and give you thevoluminous blowoutof your dreams.
When deciding what hair tool to invest in, the first thing you have to ask yourself is: “Do I wantstraight hairor curly hair?” To which T3 would say, “Why not both?” This Curling Straightener is a 2-in-1 iron so you don't have to choose. Talk about a win-win.
Yes, Dyson hair tools are included in Ulta's HAIR20 promotion, and, yes, they are worth the hype. Granted, even with the sale, a Dyson Airwrap is a serious investment, so if you're still unsure, make sure to check outour Dyson Airwrap I.D. review.
If you're looking for a more simplehairdryerthan the airwrap, but you still want salon-quality, then the Shark Speedstyle Pro Flex hair dryer system is your best bet. It's foldable design makes it easy to store and it comes with four attachments, so you can style your hair how you want it.
Made specifically for those withlow porosity, curly, or coily hair, Pattern's Hair Steamer is a great tool if you want to get the most out of your hydrating hair products, especiallyconditioners.
I don't know what it is about hair curlers, but I can never get the angle or direction quite right. More often than not, I', left with a mop of mis-matched ringlets—not a good look. So when I learned that automatic rotatingcurling ironslike this one from T3 existed, it was a game changer. You'll never have to question yourself again, just let the rotating curler do the work!

She's a film and TV obsessive who loves a good marathon almost as much as she loves a good glitter eyeshadow.

trend_name: Leighton Meester's Beauty Routine Involves a “Damage Control” Hairstyle, Exfoliating Mitts, and a $69 Date Night Fragrance
description:
*Adds to cart*

Beauty routines are integral to gettingIn The Mood—not for any other person, but for you. Here, get a glimpse into the products, habits, and mantras that help powerhouse women slip into the sexiest version of themselves.
Something I love: that last year was aLeighton Meesterrenaissance. She was inThe Buccaneers,I Love LA,andNobody Wants This, the latter of which brought inexplicable joy to every ‘90s baby. She landed a beauty partnership with Bubble, had a stint as aredhead, and, if you’ve been on TikTok, had a “Good Girls Gone Bad” viral moment.
And now that we’re firmly in 2026, it’s clear that the actress is just gaining more momentum. She has a Netflix romcom based on “The Bodyguard" coming up (release date TBD)and, most recently, landed another beauty partnership, this time as the face of Elizabeth Arden’s Eternal Aura Eau de Parfum. “The brand is such a tried-and-true for me, and they’ve been a part of my consciousness, for, well, pretty much my entire life, so this really feels like a pinch-me moment,” Meester tells me over our Zoom call this weekend.
Fragranceaside, theGossip Girlalum has many beauty secrets to share. My favorite of which: exfoliating her body like she’s “an alien coming to Earth for the first time” who needs to shed her “alien skin.” To learn how she does just that, and scoop up her other beauty tips for gettingIn the Mood,read ahead.
I used to listen to Louise Hay, who is a motivational speaker. The first thing that she says isI am worthy of love because I exist.That’s the core of all of my natural or learned insecurities and saying it means I can face the world because I am enough. At first, it felt really alien and embarrassing to say it, and I had to actually force myself to feel that way. But now I just say it naturally without having to reinforce it constantly and I do just believe it. It comes from within. I mean, love is our most core need, and to know that I am worthy of existing, and being loved, and loving myself—it matters.
My dream is to do aneverything shower, but my reality is an efficient shower. However, I take pieces of the everything shower and infuse them into my efficient shower. I’ll do adeep conditionerone day,exfoliateanother day—that way there’s not such a buildup for an everything shower. But I am actually abath person. It isn’t that I go into my bath planning to spend hours there—although I can and I will. But I’ll do work from the bath (obviously not Zooms), I’ll read, and relax. If I can get a morning bath in? Wow, it’s kind of crazy, but it helps me just seize the day.
I love anexfoliatingmitt. I love going to a spa day at Olympic Day Spa in LA, which is a Korean spa. It’s co-ed, but there’s a women’s only section. The first time I ever went I was like,what is this?They use these exfoliating mitts, and it's like if you were like an alien coming to Earth for the first time, and they're like, you need to shed your alien skin to be here. It’s amazing. I bought a version of them so I can use them at home before Ishaveor something, but I’m much gentler when I use them myself.
Get exclusive access to fashion and beauty trends, hot-off-the-press celebrity news, and more.
I like the idea of asignature scent. I’m in love with Elizabeth Arden’s Eternal Aura. I wear that regularly. I feel like it is adate-night scentbecause it gives you a sense of romance. It’s grounding, but still an uplifting, happy fragrance in my opinion. It’s not too heavy either, so I can wear it during the day.
I usetinted moisturizer,actually. I don’t think I ever usefoundationorconcealer. My skills have actually gotten worse over the years, which you would think I would have learned a thing or two. I curl my lashes and take my time with that. Then I like a brownliquid linerand something on my lips.
My hair has taken a lot of heat and color recently, so it’s pretty damaged. I went from brunette to red to blonde back tobrunettejust in the last eight months, so I’m really doing damage control. I’ve been doing oil treatments, avoiding heat, and letting itair drywhenever I can. Occasionally, I’ll use adiffuser, or I’ll put a curl in my front two pieces and grab aclaw clipfor the rest so it stays out of my face.
I love acherry red. Any time I try to do anything different, unless it’s neutral, I regret it. I just love looking down and seeing red,short nails.
I might truncate my routine, but it’s so minimal anyway because I have to pack it all the time. I’m pretty consistent with it because I have to be.Taking off my makeupis number one for me.
As more time goes by, the more hindsight I have. There’s also a confidence that comes from getting to know myself more and more, from just experience in life.
Don’t overdo alcohol and usesunscreen. The sun that I got when I was young is now starting to show up.
For more than 30 years, Marie Claire has been an internationally recognized destination for news, fashion and beauty trends, investigative packages, and more. When it comes to the products Marie Claire recommends, we take your faith in us seriously. Every product that we feature comes personally recommended by a Marie Claire writer or editor, or by an expert we’ve spoken to firsthand.
Samantha Holender is the Senior Beauty Editor at Marie Claire, where she reports on the best new launches, dives into the science behind skincare, and shares the breakdown on the latest and greatest trends in the beauty space. She's studied up on every ingredient you'll find on INCI list and is constantly in search of the world's glowiest makeup products. She's constantly tracking the biggest nail and hair trends to pop up in the beauty space, going backstage during fashion weeks, tracking celebrity looks, and constantly talking to celebrity hair stylists, nail artists, and makeup artists. Prior to joining the team, she worked asUs Weekly’s Beauty and Style Editor, where she stayed on the pulse of pop culture and broke down celebrity beauty routines, hair transformations, and red carpet looks. Her words have also appeared onPopsugar,Makeup.com,Skincare.com, Delish.com, and Philadelphia Wedding. Samantha also serves as a board member for the American Society of Magazine Editors (ASME). She first joined the organization in 2018, when she worked as an editorial intern at Food Network Magazine and Pioneer Woman Magazine. Samantha has a degree in Journalism and Mass Communications from The George Washington University’s School of Media and Public Affairs. While at GWU, she was a founding member of the school’s HerCampus chapter and served as its President for four years. When she’s not deep in the beauty closet or swatching eyeshadows, you can find her obsessing over Real Housewives and all things Bravo. Keep up with her on Instagram @samholender.

I just got back from five days ofvacation in the Caribbean, and yes, I’m still mourning the sunshine and warm island breeze. After living in New York for 13 years, I deeply understand that by the time late February or early March rolls around, you 100 percent have to book a trip somewhere warm, for both your sanity and yourskin health. For context, my complexion has been battlingeczema flaressince December, and thehormonal acnepopping up along my jawline can be at least slightly credited to stress (and the resulting cortisol spikes) so a week lying on a beach with a piña colada, asmutty romance novel, and a firm zero-email policy was everything I needed to make it through the rest of winter in one piece. But that doesn’t mean I slacked on mybeauty treatmentspre-travel. On the contrary, I’ve figured out exactly what to book before I ever set foot on the plane to make sure I still look polished, even if the only glam I get into each day is copioussunscreenapplication.
Hats off to anyone who is game to arrive at their beachside getaway with a bare face and zero pre-treatments, but I’m a longtime beauty editor with high-maintenance hair and skin, and so that will never be me. Still, I don’t exactly love the idea of applying makeup or styling mywavy 2B hairto the same degree as I do back in NYC; nor do I want to look completely blown out or ragged in my vacation photos (vanity, baby, vanity). So, like any good beauty obsessive, here’s everything I suggest doing before going on vacation to look really, really, ridiculously hot—minimal effort required.
Myeyelashesare naturally quite light with a blonde base, so not great for providing definition without mascara. I swear by a lash lift (which is basically a perm) and tint to give me that curl and mascara-level color, without any product, but before I book my appointment, I make sure that my lash density is on point.
Want thicker lashes that look like you’re wearingfalsies? Add this serum to your pre-travel routine a few weeks out and you’ll hit the beach with thicker, fuller, flirtier lashes.
If you’re looking for a prostaglandin-free option to grow your lashes, you can’t go wrong with this gentle formula. Prepare for compliments after a few weeks of use.
Myfull browsare my calling card, but again, my hair is naturally on the lighter side. If an appointment to tint and shape them is feasible, I swear byJoey Healyin Manhattan orKristie Streicherin LA, but if I have to DIY, a stellar pair oftweezersand some beard dye (yes really) are almost as good.
I know this has a steeper price tag than drugstore options, but I live and die by these Swiss-made tweezers. Those rogue hairs that pop up so far outside my desired brow shape you’d think I was a Eugene Levy double don’t stand a chance.
This is my most-beloved beauty editor hack forfuller-looking brows(even my mom swears by it). I do two five-minute sessions to tint my brows, and the results last for weeks—no brow gel or pencil required.
I color my hair withcamel-coat blonde highlightsthree times a year, but if I’m not careful, it will turn brassy in the sun. So, before I step foot on a plane, I make sure to do aglossand toning treatment to keep my blonde bright and beach-ready.
If you’re a blonde devotee like me, you know thatpurple shampooand purple masks are a must to counteract those unwanted brassy tones. This new luxe mask works so fast, one treatment is all I need pre-travel to amplify that neutral blonde shade that I love.
With copious sunshine and salt water, your hair is bound to get a little dry and damaged. I prep for that with this bonding gloss treatment, which also gives mywavy haira healthy boost of shine.
I’m a true sunshine baby, but I know better than to sacrifice my health and skin condition for a tan, especially whenself-tannerin 2026 is so exceptional. I reach for my favorite formulas a few days before vacation for a gorgeous faux glow.
The entireMCbeauty teamswears by this tanning milkand for good reason—it’s an incredibly easy way to build a natural-looking tan without streaks or that tell-tale orange cast. I make sure to get three applications in (post-shower and exfoliating) for a healthy tan color.
If I’m short on prep days or I want a deeper, richer tan, I apply 1-2 coats of this tanning mousse with a mitt (plus a dense brush for my wrists and fingers). After a few hours of developing, I’m a buttery biscuit bronze and ready forvacation.
I’m a few years into professional hair removal for mybikini lineandunderarms, but pre-vacation I make sure to give myself a little touch-up with an at-homehair removal deviceto nix any strays.
Get exclusive access to fashion and beauty trends, hot-off-the-press celebrity news, and more.
The rotating head and three different attachments on this device make it super easy to reach every area that needs a hair removal touch-up. It’s also tested and safe for all skin types and tones.
This is my go-to IPL device for the bikini line, and after five days of wearing my skimpiestbikinison the beach, I can safely say that I looked smooth and stubble-free.

Hannah Baxteris the Beauty Director at Marie Claire. She has previously held roles atThe Zoe Report, Coveteur, andBust Magazine,covering beauty, wellness, fashion, and lifestyle. She authors theMarie ClairenewsletterFace Forward. Her writing has appeared inHarper's Bazaar, Allure, The Cut, Elle, InStyle, Glamour, Air Mail, Vogue, Architectural Digest, Byrdie, Nylonand more.
She is also the founder ofAnxiety Beer, a weekly newsletter about the intersection of culture and mental health. In her spare time you can catch her reading too many overdue library books, thrifting, or hanging with her hairless cat, Norman. You can find her onInstagramandTikTok@hannahbaxward.

It happened: I forgot to shop the President's Daybeauty sales. Luckily, my collection of makeup andskincare productswill still get a boost on a budget thanks to the beauty sales thatarestill ongoing. Of course, discounted products during non-traditional sale times can be hard to sort through, especially when you want to get the best quality for the biggest discount. To help you sift through the good, the bad, and the unnecessary, I combed through sale sections atUlta,Sephora, and other top retailers to find the 10 best beauty products on sale this week.
Looking for a new signatureperfume for 2026? There's a discount for that. Hoping to to restock on your favorite lash serum? There's a discount for that (courtesy ofUlta's National Lash Day sale on lash products, which ends on Feb. 22). Finally ready to splurge on thatmulti-styler hair tool? There's a discount for that, too. You just have to know where to look.
So, get your wallet ready and be prepared to act fast—these deals won't last forever, and some in-demand products might sell out fast. Here are the 10 best beauty products on sale this week that are worth the spend.
Finally, a high-endmascarathat actually stays put. Lancôme's Hypnôse Drama Waterproof mascara brings the volumeandstays on no matter what life throws at you.
This beloved lash-enhancing serum isn't cheap, so if you're already a fan, I highly recommend splurging while it's on sale. This tube will last you six months and is currently 30 percent off.
This holiday set from MAC was already a pretty good deal at it's original $42 price, but at $25.20, it's a real steal. The set comes with a full-size Glow Play Cushiony Blush and a GlowPlay TenderTalk Lip Balm, which originally retails for $26, meaning you're basically buying alip balmand getting a sheer, soft blush for free.
MAC's Pro Longwear Paint Pots are a classic for a reason. You can use it as a one-and-done product or as the base for a more elaborate eye look.
If the cold temperatures have been drying out your skin, then it might be worth investing in amoisturizing mask. This one from Murad acts like a night moisturizer—no rinse off, just plump, hydrated skin.
About-face's Cheek Freak Blush Balm is the perfectblushfor anyone that enjoys vibrant colors and wants their makeup to stay on all day long. The pigment in these is no joke and it's super easy to use.
The Performer is one of the bestfoundationsfor dry-combination skin and comes in an impressive shade range with many different undertones. Pro tip: grab it while your shade is still in stock.
This beloved hair mask is currently 25 percent off at DermStreet with codeLOVE25and it's a must if you're suffering with dry, damaged hair.
Layering fragrancesis definitely a trend I'm bringing into 2026, and what better way to start playing around with your signature scent than with a trio of travel sized perfumes from Boy Smells? A set like this is great for those who want to experiment and try new fragrances without committing to a full-size bottle.
This multi-use hot tool comes with everything you need to get the blowout of your dreams. The Shark FlexStyle comes with multiple attachments that make experimenting with your style easy. And, it'sMarie Claire-approved.

With winter still raging on, thanks to Winter Storm Hernando, my skin is in need of some major TLC. Cold temperatures and chilly winds are enough to make even the mostoily skinfeel dry and tight. And the blizzard-friendly weather is making me reach for myfavorite hydrating productsfordry skinmore often than normal, which means I'm running out faster than anticipated—I need a restock. Fast. Luckily, thanks to the best beauty sales of the week, I can replenish my skincare cabinetandsave a little money.
BothLaneigeand Fresh—two skincare brands with some of my favorite products for dry skin—are currently having Friends & Family sales, meaning you can splurge without the guilt. If you havesensitive skinand like to avoid fragrance, then you'll want Laneige to be your first stop. If, on the contrary, scented products make your skincare routine feel extra special, then Fresh products, with their vibrant-but-not-artificial scents, are more than definitely worth trying.
Not shopping for skincare this week? No worries. Laura Mercier,Ulta, and Dermstore also have selections on sale this week, and I've combed through all the discounts to find the best deals and products worth your hard-earned cash. These are the best beauty products to buy on sale the week of February 23.
Ulta is always running some kind of sale—and if you have the patients to sort through pages and pages of discounts, you can find some hidden gems.
PMD's Microdermabrasion tool is a great tool for those hoping to get powerfulexfoliationwithout having to go to the spa. If you're new to using at-home tools, don't worry, this one is extremely user-friendly.
Londontown's Lakurnail polishis an absolute favorite of mine. The colors are vibrant and bright and, when applied properly, it will look the same on day seven as it did on day one.
Regular brush maintenance is a must for any self-respecting makeup lover. Having back-up sponges always encourages me to replace my tools regularly.
Dermstore is my go-to for majority of my skincare shopping. They have so many derm-backed brands, and a lot of products on the site are HSA-eligible.
Looking to add someniacinamideto your skincare routine? This duo from Paula's Choice comes with a niacinamide toner and serum to improve texture and lessen the look of pores.
I loveeye masks, but most of the ones I try end up slipping off my face—except these from Wander Beauty.
Every winter like clockwork, my hair becomes a static mess, which is why I rely on hair oils like this one by Christophe Robin to tame my flyawaysandkeep me from shocking myself every five seconds.
Laneige is currently having a Friends & Family sale, with 25 percent off of select skincare products, including the beloved Laneige Lip Sleeping Mask. No code needed.
The Laneige Lip Sleeping Mask is what put the brand on the map, and for good reason. This moisturizing balm is perfect for keeping your lips smooth and soft. Best of all? Every shade is currently on sale, even limited edition ones like Matcha Bubble Tea.
The Laneige Cream Toner is another one of the brand's signature products thanks to its ceramide and peptide complex, which will give your skin an instant hydration boost upon application.
As someone who often struggles withred skin, I love the Laneige Cica Sleeping Mask. Not only is the moisturizer thick enough to soothe dry skin, it also includes the soothing antioxidant Centella Asiatica, which helps calm redness while you sleep.
A donut-inspired lip serum in a creamy baby pink? Say less. This Glaze Crazy Tinted Lip Serum gives high shine and pigment for that juicy, plump look.
When coming up with a skincare routine, it can be easy to forget about your eyes, but as I've gotten older, I've started noticing that the skin around my eyes can get really dry and flaky. Aneye creamlike this one is crucial to my winter skincare routine.
Fresh is currently treating customers to 25 percent off site-wide, with 30 percent off last chance products.
Dry skin and aclay masknormally don't mix, but in my experience, the Umbrian Clay Purifying Mask from Fresh is the exception. I've been using this mask for a decade and it always leaves my skin feeling refreshed—not stripped. It's great for combo skin and will definitely come in handy as the temperatures start to climb in the spring and summer.
This moisturizer is thick to the touch, but won't leave your skin feeling heavy. It's known for leaving the skin feeling silky and smooth.
Splurging on aface washisn't for everyone, but if you like to treat yourself to a little bit of luxury every morning and night, then this Soy Face Cleanser from Fresh is the perfect indulgence. It's ahydrating gel cleanserthat won't strip your skin, creating a nice base for the rest of your skincare routine.
Laura Mercier is also having a friends and family anniversary sale. Customers get 20 percent off select products with the code “LMUSANNI20.”
Laura Mercier's loosesetting powderis a must-try for anyone getting into makeup, especially if you haveoily skin. This talc-free formula comes in three sheer shades—Translucent, Translucent Honey, Translucent Medium-Deep—and one brightening shade—Tone-Up. Set your makeup with this powder, and you won't need a touch-up for hours.
Another cult favorite from Laura Mercier is the Tinted Moisturizer. The brand claims sheer coverage, but this foundation can easily be layered for medium coverage. It also comes in 20 flexible shades and has SPF 30.
Thiseyelineris great for defining the eyes thanks to its smooth, gel-like texture. Just beware, reviewers warn it's not easy to blend out once applied.
